name: Urolithin A improved muscle strength and mitochondrial biomarkers in middle-aged adults but missed the primary power endpoint
summary: ATLAS adds a second human functional and biomarker signal for urolithin A, while preserving a primary-endpoint boundary.
statement: In 88 untrained overweight middle-aged adults, 4 months of urolithin A significantly improved muscle strength by about 12%, lowered plasma acylcarnitines and C-reactive protein, and increased skeletal-muscle proteins linked to mitophagy and mitochondrial metabolism, but did not significantly improve the primary peak-power endpoint.
quantitative_note: The abstract reports about 12% muscle-strength improvement, clinically meaningful VO2 and 6-minute-walk movement, significant acylcarnitine and C-reactive protein lowering, and no significant improvement in peak power output.
caveats: The primary peak-power endpoint was not significant.
Participants were middle-aged and overweight, not frail older adults or a broad aging population.
Muscle biopsy proteomic signals support mitophagy-linked pathway engagement but do not prove mitophagy causality for functional changes.
The sponsor employed several authors and funded the study.
